{
  "task_echo": "The user needs to decide between an Electron app or a local web server (aiki serve) to visualize their multi-LLM orchestration CLI for a hackathon, and requires standout features and a development plan. The constraints include a one-week deadline and a solo developer working with existing locally installed CLIs.",
  "strongest_version": "The strongest version of this idea is the `aiki serve` local web server approach. By embedding a local Node.js server and SPA within the existing npm package, current users can instantly access a rich web dashboard with zero friction. It avoids the massive overhead and packaging nightmares of Electron distribution while cleanly handling local CLI spawning and WebSocket streaming. This allows a solo developer to focus their limited one-week timeline entirely on building an impressive 'Council Debate' visualization that showcases parallel AI reasoning, delivering a high 'wow factor' for the hackathon without installation headaches.",

      "stance": "SUPPORT",
      "basis": "INFERENCE",
      "nature": "JUDGMENT",

      "output": "DECISION",
      "title": "Select `aiki serve` over Electron",
      "detail": "Embed a lightweight Express/Vite server into the existing npm CLI to serve a local web interface.",
      "user_value": "Zero-friction access to a GUI without a separate large binary download.",
      "why_distinctive": "It builds directly on the user's existing distribution channel (npm) and respects the 1-week timeline.",

        "E-4"
